input_roles.main_reference_image: Provides the visual style, subject identity, composition elements, scene facts, lighting logic, costume, props and narrative state that must remain unchanged.
input_roles.supporting_reference_images: Only supplement secondary angle, local detail, material, lighting or composition evidence; never override the main reference image.

must_preserve: same image style and cinematic color language
same subject identity and body/costume/prop information
same scene identity, environment facts and era logic
same key composition elements and spatial relationships
same lighting direction, atmosphere and material logic
same narrative moment without adding new story events
may_vary: visual center within the same scene facts

layout.sheet_intent: nine-panel cinematic shot exploration for the same reference image facts
layout.grid: strict 3x3 with uniform gutters, clean panel divisions and consistent color management
layout.panel_order: left-to-right, top-to-bottom; each panel is one distinct shot size or camera view

panel_label_policy.display_strategy: short_ascii_shot_label_in_image, post_overlay_required_if image text is unreliable
panel_label_policy.fallback: reserve a clean lower-left label strip in every panel

shot_plan.label: P1 EST
shot_plan.camera_view: neutral eye-level or slightly high angle
shot_plan.purpose: establishes the full scene, subject placement, atmosphere and major composition elements from the reference

shot_plan.label: P2 WIDE
shot_plan.purpose: shows subject-to-environment relationship while preserving the reference style and spatial anchors

shot_plan.label: P3 FULL
shot_plan.purpose: proves the full subject silhouette, costume, props and posture without redesign

shot_plan.label: P4 MED
shot_plan.purpose: centers the subject action or expression while retaining background and composition cues

shot_plan.label: P5 CU
shot_plan.camera_view: stable lens with shallow depth emphasis if appropriate
shot_plan.purpose: preserves face/object identity and emotional focus from the same reference moment

shot_plan.label: P6 ECU
shot_plan.purpose: shows key texture, prop, eye, hand, material or symbolic detail already present in the reference

shot_plan.label: P7 OTS
shot_plan.camera_view: foreground layer framing the subject or scene
shot_plan.purpose: adds cinematic depth and foreground/midground/background layering without changing facts

shot_plan.label: P8 LOW
shot_plan.purpose: emphasizes scale, power, verticality or tension while preserving identity and style

shot_plan.label: P9 HIGH
shot_plan.camera_view: high-angle, overhead-leaning or subjective POV when suitable
shot_plan.purpose: reveals spatial order, vulnerability, surveillance or subjective viewing within the same scene

critical_requirements: Keep the reference image style, composition elements, subject information, costume, props, environment and narrative facts unchanged.
Design exactly one 3x3 cinematic shot grid, not nine unrelated images.
Every panel must be a different cinematic shot size or camera view from the same scene and subject.
Use common film language: establishing, wide, full, medium, close-up, extreme close-up, over-shoulder, low-angle, high-angle or POV.
Do not redesign the subject, location, costume, props, lighting logic or visual style.
Every panel must include a short lower-left shot label or preserve a clean overlay-ready label strip.
negative_constraints: no new costume, prop, logo, weapon or architecture
no different location or unrelated background
no style transfer, no color palette drift
no comic page, no storyboard sequence with new actions, no poster collage

prompt_assembly.final_prompt_template: Create one CINEMATIC_SHOT_GRID as a strict 3x3 nine-panel image based on the reference image(s) and <user_desc>. Preserve the reference image style, composition elements, subject identity, costume, props, environment, lighting logic and narrative facts unchanged. Each panel must show the same subject and scene through a different common film shot size or camera view: P1 EST, P2 WIDE, P3 FULL, P4 MED, P5 CU, P6 ECU, P7 OTS, P8 LOW, P9 HIGH/POV. Keep all panels visually consistent, cinematic, and label-ready.

review_focus: same reference image style across all panels
same subject identity and scene facts across all panels
clear 3x3 grid with nine distinct cinematic shot sizes or camera views
no subject, prop, costume, environment or lighting redesign
lower-left shot labels or overlay-ready label strips
